segments.title: JMSDF destroyers grew through continuous construction
segments.summary: The episode frames Japan's Cold War destroyer force as a steady, iterative build-up from borrowed US hulls to a large modern JMSDF surface fleet.
segments.body: Japan rebuilt naval capability by keeping destroyer construction moving, initially through politically acceptable ASW escorts and then through larger command, helicopter, and missile ships.

evidence.note: The opening frames Japan's naval strength as the result of repeatedly building destroyers, starting with transferred Fletcher and Gleaves-class ships.
The Ayanami discussion links modest ship size, caution about not alarming neighbors, and the emergence of Japanese shipbuilding practice.
The conclusion says Japan grew from four destroyers toward a large surface-combatant force through constant construction, improvement, and industrial investment.
